sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! After clicking the purple ring at (38, 47):
- White is now at (38, 47) area (shown as 3 separate W pixels at (36,47), (37,46), (38,45) - looks like the white diamond is moving/changing shape)
- Purple ring moved from (38, 47) to (39, 48)
- Dark gray at (41, 49) now has 6 pixels (was 12)
- Dark gray at (35, 45) disappeared
So the pattern continues:
- Visit all dark gray diamonds
- Click purple ring → white moves to purple ring position, purple ring moves, dark gray diamonds change
Current state:
- White at ~ (38, 47) area
- Orange ring at (9, 30)
- Purple ring at (39, 48)
- Dark gray diamonds at: (6, 17), (9, 49), (12, 24), (41, 49) with 6 pixels
Let me continue the pattern - visit all dark gray diamonds and click purple ring again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Visit all 4 dark gray diamonds then click purple ring
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 6, 'col': 17}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 9, 'col': 49}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 12, 'col': 24}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 49}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
w_pos = None
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        w_pos = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
print(f"After 4 visits: white at {w_pos}")

# Click purple ring at (39, 48)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 48}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("After purple ring click:")
for n in seg:
    if n['pixels'] < 30 and n['color'] in ['W', 'G', 'p', 'O', 'M']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
